summary: This cluster investigates the molecular physiology of dinoflagellates, specifically through the lens of their ion channel and signaling repertoires. A central finding is the reconstruction of a consensus "channelome" from 27 transcriptomes, which identified 36 ion channel families, including homologs of animal and plant proteins alongside unique protist-specific channels. The research highlights the remarkable physiological adaptability of dinoflagellates, driven by extensive G-protein coupled receptor and Ca2+-dependent signaling pathways. The work is primarily bioinformatic and transcriptomic in scope, focusing on broad physiological mechanisms rather than a specific geographic or ecological context.
